judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After filtering NPM packages by MIT license and IsRelease in packageinfo, the agent tried an exact-equality join between project_packageversion.ProjectName and the free-text project_info.Project_Information (call_Q9bQcPEbRiW5B8WgZkOatzv5 returned []), then concluded it couldn't proceed due to unstructured text instead of using a substring/text-parse join and extracting fork counts (and also misapplied the MIT filter to package licenses rather than project licenses).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly joining MIT-release NPM packages to GitHub projects, the agent limited the candidates to the first 200 (head(200)) and then used a regex that only captured phrases like "123 forks" (missing the frequent "forks count of 123" format) when parsing project_info, so it extracted almost no valid fork counts and incorrectly concluded the data was insufficient.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python, the agent extracted fork counts from project_info.Project_Information using the regex r"(\d+) forks", which misses the prevalent "forks count of NNNN" phrasing shown in the trace, so many high-fork projects were not captured and the top-5 ranking was wrong.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered by the package's Licenses field in package_database (var_call_tuzyC6ONetjDYkHIjZO2yZHO) and never filtered project_info by project license, then parsed forks from Project_Information (var_call_dMvO3SNTezyLaOK8UqS6wCz5), so it used the wrong data source for the 'project license MIT' constraint and returned an incorrect top 5.

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent tried to join project_packageversion with project_info on a nonexistent column (pi.ProjectName), then incorrectly attempted an exact match between pp.ProjectName and the free-text pi.Project_Information (yielding an empty result), and prematurely concluded the task was impossible instead of parsing Project_Information to extract repository names and fork counts as required by the schema.
B. Failure modes encountered:
